name: Yahoo Finance: News Headlines
summary: Latest Yahoo Finance news headlines for a given ticker.
description: Fetch the latest Yahoo Finance news headlines for a ticker. Returns headline, URL, publisher, and relative timestamp. Pure read; complements finance/stock-price (quote) since news is a separate output contract.

limit.description: Maximum number of headlines to return. Default 20; capped at the number Yahoo renders without scrolling (typically 20–25).

ticker.description: Same ticker grammar as finance/stock-price (AAPL, ^GSPC, BTC-USD, EURUSD=X, BMW.DE, etc.).

steps.name: Navigate to Yahoo news for ticker

inputs.script: const cleanArg = (v) => { const t = String(v == null ? '' : v).trim(); return /^\{[a-zA-Z0-9_]+\}$/.test(t) ? '' : t; }; const ticker = cleanArg(arg0); if (!ticker) throw new Error('ticker is required'); const limitRaw = cleanArg(arg1); const limit = limitRaw && /^[1-9][0-9]*$/.test(limitRaw) ? parseInt(limitRaw, 10) : 20; const norm = (v) => { const t = String(v || '').replace(/\s+/g, ' ').trim(); return t || 'N/A'; }; const items = Array.from(document.querySelectorAll("[data-testid='storyitem']")); if (!items.length) throw new Error('No story items found for ' + ticker); const extractPubAge = (item) => { const pub = item.querySelector('.publishing'); if (!pub) return { publisher: '', age: '' }; const parts = (pub.innerText || '').split(/\n+/).map((s) => s.trim()).filter((s) => s && s !== '\u2022'); if (parts.length >= 2) return { publisher: parts[0], age: parts[parts.length - 1] }; if (parts.length === 1) { const m = parts[0].split(/\s*\u2022\s*/).map((s) => s.trim()).filter(Boolean); if (m.length >= 2) return { publisher: m[0], age: m[m.length - 1] }; return { publisher: parts[0], age: '' }; } const textNodes = Array.from(pub.childNodes).filter((n) => n.nodeType === 3).map((n) => n.textContent.trim()).filter(Boolean); return { publisher: textNodes[0] || '', age: textNodes[textNodes.length - 1] || '' }; }; const headlines = items.slice(0, limit).map((item) => { const link = item.querySelector('a[href]'); const headlineText = item.querySelector('h3, h4, [class*="headline"]')?.textContent || link?.getAttribute('title') || link?.getAttribute('aria-label') || link?.textContent || ''; const { publisher, age } = extractPubAge(item); return { title: norm(headlineText), url: link?.href || 'N/A', publisher: norm(publisher), age: norm(age) }; }).filter((h) => h.title !== 'N/A'); return { ticker, count: headlines.length, headlines };

help.summary: Latest Yahoo Finance news headlines for a given ticker.

parameters.limit: Maximum number of headlines to return. Default 20; capped at the number Yahoo renders without scrolling (typically 20–25).
parameters.ticker: Same ticker grammar as finance/stock-price (AAPL, ^GSPC, BTC-USD, EURUSD=X, BMW.DE, etc.).

examples.command: rzn-browser run finance yahoo-news --param ticker="^GSPC" --param limit="5"

returns: Object: { ticker, count, headlines: [{ title, url, publisher, age }] }. Pair with finance/stock-price for current quote on the same ticker.
notes: JS-first: reads [data-testid='storyitem']. Yahoo has used this contract for several redesigns.
Publisher and age come from the '.publishing' div inside each story card (first/last non-bullet text node, separated by a U+2022 bullet).
Publisher and timestamp fields can still come back as 'N/A' on some story types (sponsored content, video-only items).
